screenshot_dashboard: dashboard screenshot unavailable — Chromium thread exhaustion in sandbox (pthread_create EAGAIN); desktop+mobile captured at 15:51 UTC

suggestions.title: NEW CRITICAL: Activity log permanently empty on live site — JSON format mismatch
suggestions.description: activity.json returns {"entries": [...]} — an object, not an array. App.jsx syncFromGitHub does: setActivityLog(Array.isArray(activity) ? activity : []). Since the payload is an object, Array.isArray is false, and activityLog is unconditionally set to [] every poll. No error thrown, no activityError set — HTTP fetch succeeds (200 OK). Users see 'No activity yet…' forever, with zero indication. 50 entries silently discarded every 30s. Fix: setActivityLog(Array.isArray(activity) ? activity : (activity.entries ?? [])). One-line change.

suggestions.title: NEW CRITICAL: workers.json missing `id` field — React key chaos and custom avatar art degraded to emoji
suggestions.description: Live workers.json has no `id` field on worker objects (confirmed on GitHub raw). Cascading failures: (1) key={w.id} at App.jsx lines 1112, 1125 = key={undefined} — React key collision, undefined behavior in DOM reconciliation. (2) CharacterAvatar receives workerId={undefined}, misses all specific-worker SVG branches, falls to if(emoji) branch — users see 🎨 or 🔍 emoji glyphs instead of the hand-crafted SVG character portraits. The custom art is in the code but invisible on production. (3) handleWorkerClick calls setFocusedWorker(worker.id) = setFocusedWorker(undefined). On next render, focusedWorker===w.id = undefined===undefined = TRUE for ALL workers simultaneously. Clicking any character focuses all cards at once. Fix: add id field to workers.json, or normalize in syncFromGitHub: data.workers.map(w => ({ id: w.name?.toLowerCase(), ...w })).

suggestions.title: NEW: workers.json role uses short names — ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS lookups silently fail
suggestions.description: Live workers.json has role: 'frontend' for Maya. ROLE_EMOJIS and ROLE_COLORS are keyed by full names ('Frontend Engineer', 'Backend Engineer', etc.). ROLE_EMOJIS['frontend'] is undefined → getRoleEmoji returns '🤖' fallback. ROLE_COLORS['frontend'] is undefined → progress ring, hover card, desk lamp all use gray (#6b7280) instead of the correct purple accent. The custom color theming per role is invisible on production. Fix: normalize role field in fetchWorkersFromGitHub, or add a role-alias map in constants.js, and document the expected contract.

suggestions.title: NEW: No ErrorBoundary — any thrown render error = white screen with no recovery path
suggestions.description: Zero error boundary coverage in App.jsx, Office.jsx, or main.jsx. Risk surfaces: CharacterAvatar renders dynamic SVG per worker ID; ProgressBar does Date arithmetic on worker.startedAt; ActivityLogEntry calls roster?.find(); vibe streak effect reads and JSON.parses localStorage. Any one throws on malformed data and the entire React tree crashes to a blank white screen. Add a 20-line ErrorBoundary class component with componentDidCatch and a fallback UI with a retry button. Wrap it around at minimum the Office component and the dashboard worker grid.

suggestions.title: 25 duplicate @keyframes across App.css and Office.css — persists unchanged since review #18
suggestions.description: Exact duplicate keyframe names confirmed: ambient-drift, badge-pop, bob, card-rise, cloud-drift, deep-glow, dust-drift, enter-office, fade-in-char, ghost-enter, ghost-float, manager-wobble, monitor-blink, new-border-pulse, plant-sway, pulse, pulse-live, shimmer, standby-pulse, star-twinkle, typing-bounce, wander-1 through wander-4 (25 total). Each block is parsed and stored twice. Built CSS total is ~90KB. Extract shared keyframes to src/styles/keyframes.css and import from both. This has been flagged in reviews 16, 17, 18, and now 19. It remains completely unaddressed.

suggestions.title: Four conditional aria-live mounts still broken — screen readers get no announcements
suggestions.description:
All four still conditionally mount with content pre-populated: (1) completionToast (App.jsx ~955), (2) plantToast (Office.jsx ~948), (3) ShortcutToast div (App.jsx ~543), (4) polling-paused-pill span (App.jsx ~1007). WCAG 4.1.3: the live region must exist before content changes. A conditionally-mounted element with aria-live bypasses screen reader mutation observers. Fix: add a persistent
to App root. Expose a useAnnounce() hook that sets its textContent imperatively. Four callers replace conditional JSX mounting with announce('message'). One hook, four violations resolved.

suggestions.title: activity.json entries use undocumented type values — 🔧 fallback shown for all real events
suggestions.description: ACTIVITY_ICONS maps: hire, complete, error, system. Live activity.json uses types: deploy, review, complete, hire. Types 'deploy' and 'review' are not in ACTIVITY_ICONS — render as 🔧 fallback. If the activity log is ever un-broken (finding #1), all deploy and review entries would show a wrench. Fix: add deploy: '🚀' and review: '🔍' to ACTIVITY_ICONS, or normalize types in fetchActivityFromGitHub.

suggestions.title: stableActivityLog useMemo is a no-op — WorkerCard memo bypassed every poll
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 759: const stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog]). The dependency IS the value. The memo fires every time activityLog changes (every 30s poll), returning the same activityLog reference anyway. Zero stability benefit. WorkerCard is React.memo'd but receives a new activityEntries reference every 30s, defeating the memo comparison. Fix: useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog.length]) — only new reference when count changes. One-word fix. Flagged in reviews 15–18.

suggestions.title: confettiActive setTimeout has no cleanup ref — double-trigger drops first burst mid-animation
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 852: setTimeout(() => setConfettiActive(false), 2500) — ID not stored. If vibe rank jumps twice within 2.5s, the first timer still fires and cuts off the second confetti burst. Compare to workerConfettiTimerRef used correctly 6 lines later. Store in confettiTimerRef.current (add a ref), clear before setting on re-trigger. Also: if component ever unmounts (currently won't, but as a pattern), no cleanup path exists.

suggestions.title: Ghost character divs still keyboard-focusable with no action
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 525: tabIndex={0} unconditional on all Character divs. Ghost-variant characters have no onClick (handleClick is undefined), no role='button', no onKeyDown. Keyboard users tab into ghost characters, get aria labels announced, press Enter/Space — nothing happens. Dead tab stop. Fix: tabIndex={handleClick ? 0 : -1}. Ghost OOO bubble on focus still works via onFocus.

suggestions.title: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS and getHour defined inside component functions — allocated on every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 390: const IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS = {1: '☕', 2: '💬', 3: '🪟', 4: '🧘'} is inside Character. Five Character instances × every re-render = new object allocation per render. Same for const getHour = () => new Date().getHours() inside WindowElement (line 603). Both are static, have no dependency on props or state. Hoist to module scope.

suggestions.title: vibeHistory sparkline blank on page load for returning users
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 581: useState([]) for vibeHistory. Sparkline only populates after first poll + teamVibe effect, which can take 2–30s. Data is already in localStorage. Fix: useState(() => { try { const r = localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-history'); return r ? JSON.parse(r) : [] } catch { return [] } }). Returning users see historical vibe trend immediately instead of an empty chart.

suggestions.title: Stuck worker stat card click-only — keyboard users locked out of scroll-to-worker feature
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 426–440: stat-card--stuck div has onClick with no role, tabIndex, or onKeyDown. Keyboard users cannot use this scroll-to-worker navigation affordance. Fix: add role='button', tabIndex={0}, onKeyDown that fires the scroll handler on Enter or Space.

suggestions.title: activityLogLenRef tracked but never read — confirmed dead code
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 758–760: const activityLogLenRef = useRef(0) and a useEffect that writes to it. The ref is never read anywhere in 1209 lines. Was presumably an abandoned optimization attempt. Delete both lines.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast use large inline style objects — theming and a11y hazard
suggestions.description: App.jsx ~955 and Office.jsx ~948: both toast elements use inline style={} with hardcoded background, border, borderRadius, padding, zIndex, animation. Cannot be targeted by prefers-reduced-motion, cannot use CSS custom properties for theming. Extract to .completion-toast and .plant-toast CSS classes. Only dynamic value (border color from worker role color) stays inline.

suggestions.title: Three near-identical time formatters in two files — utility drift
suggestions.description: Office.jsx defines formatIdleDuration (compact: '<1m', '45m', '2h 15m'). App.jsx defines getRelativeTime ('just now', '45m ago') and formatDuration ('Working for 45m'). All compute elapsed ms from a timestamp, outputting slightly different strings. None live in src/utils/. Add src/utils/time.js with a shared base function. Next time an edge case is found (0ms case, locale formatting), it gets fixed in one place.

suggestions.title: DeltaBadge arrows render without accessible text label
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 364–371: DeltaBadge renders ↑3 or ↓2 as raw Unicode. Screen readers announce 'up arrow 3'. Add aria-label='increased by 3' (or decreased) to the span element and aria-hidden='true' to the glyph.

suggestions.title: Worker card flip does not move focus to Close button — keyboard users navigate blind
suggestions.description: WorkerCard: clicking the info button flips to activity history back view. Focus stays on the now-visually-hidden front button (tabIndex={-1} set but no focus transfer). Keyboard users must Tab forward to find Close. Fix: add a ref to the Close button, call .focus() in a useEffect when isFlipped becomes true.

suggestions.title: 19 continuously-animating ambient particles lack will-change: transform
suggestions.description: App.jsx renders 12 .ambient-particle spans; Office.jsx renders 7 more. All run indefinite CSS animations with no will-change hint. Add will-change: transform to .ambient-particle and .char--wander-1 through -4 in CSS to promote them to GPU compositing layers. Real improvement on mid-range Android devices.

whats_good: Office floor view is genuinely beautiful on desktop. Dark grid, vibe-reactive border glow, desk lamps, window element, coffee corner, and conference table compose well. The overall art direction is consistently excellent.

Bloberto's SVG avatar renders correctly (hardcoded id in DEFAULT_BLOBERTO). The crown + blob + per-vibe facial expressions (beaming grin with star sparkles for crushing, sweat droplet for on-fire, droopy eyelids for slow-day) are impressive craft.
Vibe-reactive theming is comprehensive: CVO nameplate, trophy on desk, 6-chair conference table, COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT label, weather icon. Every UI surface responds to the same vibe data.
ShortcutToast first-time-user experience works correctly on desktop — keyboard shortcuts shown once, auto-dismissed in 7s, localStorage persistence prevents repeat shows. Clean interaction design.
Polling pause on 10-minute inactivity with 15-second check interval and resume-on-interaction is thoughtful and correct. Prevents unnecessary GitHub API calls for idle users.
ProgressBar milestone flash (25/50/75/100%) uses a Set-based tracker to prevent duplicate triggers and properly cleans up via useEffect return. Solid implementation detail.
prefers-reduced-motion coverage is comprehensive — 16 override blocks across both CSS files. Most animated elements have explicit no-animation fallbacks.
Tab keyboard navigation (ARIA tablist/tab/tabpanel roles, roving tabIndex, ArrowLeft/Right handler, hash sync) is correctly and completely implemented.
summary: Score drops to 3.1 from 3.3. Three new critical production bugs discovered this review: (1) Activity log is permanently empty on live site — activity.json returns {entries:[...]} but code does Array.isArray(activity) ? activity : [] and never reads the entries key; 50 entries silently discarded every poll cycle. (2) workers.json has no id field — React key={undefined} on all worker cards, CharacterAvatar falls to emoji glyphs instead of custom SVG portraits, and clicking any worker in the office view focuses ALL cards simultaneously (undefined===undefined). (3) workers.json role uses short names ('frontend') not full names ('Frontend Engineer') — ROLE_COLORS and ROLE_EMOJIS lookups return undefined, all custom role theming falls back to gray/robot. Four data-layer fixes (add id, normalize role, change entries wrapper to array in activity.json, add activity type entries) would restore the majority of broken production features in under an hour. The code architecture and visual design remain genuinely excellent — the vibe system, Bloberto's expressive SVG art, and per-worker desk screens are high-quality work. The gap between the code's potential and what users actually experience is growing with each review.
